mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 4.0% signals labor market deterioration that immediately triggers risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in 70% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE is below 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that sustains corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when RSAFS and HOUST move in opposite directions, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints that compress profit margins, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods produce positive or flat 20-day SPX returns, or the mechanism shows no directional bias outside the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product establishes a baseline earnings trajectory that supports equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns across multiple quarters, or Rank IC remains negative over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.4% monthly threshold forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward 60-day returns are positive in the majority of months where CPIAUCSL rises above 0.4%, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market absorption that boosts consumer spending expectations, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal generates negative 20-day returns when payrolls rise but unemployment also rises, or the interaction term loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in retail sales during a late-cycle expansion signals weakening consumer demand that precedes earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: RSAFS declines in late-cycle periods produce positive 5-day SPX returns, or the signal performs identically across early-cycle and late-cycle regimes.
